name: Two Pointers, One Pass
summary: Advance fast n nodes ahead of slow, then move both until fast hits the end — slow now sits just before the node to remove.
visualizationIntent: init: build the list with a dummy sentinel (special) before the head; slow and fast both start on the dummy. phase 1 (open the gap): fast advances n nodes so a fixed n-node gap sits between slow and fast. phase 2 (slide together): slow and fast advance in lockstep until fast reaches the last node; slow now sits just before the target. update: the node after slow is the one to remove — it flashes, slow's next is rewired to skip it (a changed link flashes), and the removed node dims out (error). return: traverse from the dummy's next and show the remaining values.

lineExplanations.4: Start the next-pointer array (nextp[i] = the id node i points to).
lineExplanations.5: Loop over every real node to wire the chain.
lineExplanations.6: Every node except the last points to the following node.
lineExplanations.7: Point node i at node i+1.
lineExplanations.9: The last real node points at nothing (-1).
lineExplanations.10: Start the display-values array (parallels the nodes, plus the dummy).
lineExplanations.11: Copy each real node's value for display.
lineExplanations.14: The dummy node's id sits just past the real nodes.
lineExplanations.15: The dummy points at the head (node 0).
lineExplanations.16: changed marks whichever link just changed (for the animation).
lineExplanations.18: fast starts on the dummy too.
lineExplanations.19: Track how many steps fast has advanced.
lineExplanations.20: Advance fast n times to open the gap.
lineExplanations.23: Now slide both until fast is at the last node.
lineExplanations.26: removed is the node right after slow — the one to delete.
lineExplanations.27: Unlink it: slow's next skips over the removed node.
lineExplanations.29: The new head is whatever the dummy now points to.
lineExplanations.30: Start an empty list to read out the remaining values.
lineExplanations.31: Begin the read-out at the head.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def removeNthFromEnd(self, values, n): values are the node values; n counts from the end.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(sz): loops over each real node index.
syntaxExplanations.6: if i < sz - 1: true for every node except the last.
syntaxExplanations.7: nextp.append(i + 1) links node i to node i+1.
syntaxExplanations.8: else: handles the last node (bare else — no step of its own).
syntaxExplanations.9: nextp.append(-1) marks the tail's next as none.
syntaxExplanations.11: for i in range(sz): loops over each real node.
syntaxExplanations.12: disp_vals.append(values[i]) copies the value for display.
syntaxExplanations.13: disp_vals.append(0) adds the dummy sentinel's value.
syntaxExplanations.14: dummy = sz is the dummy node's id (just past the real nodes).
syntaxExplanations.15: nextp.append(0 if sz > 0 else -1) points the dummy at the head.
syntaxExplanations.16: changed = [] holds the id of the link that just changed.
syntaxExplanations.17: slow = dummy starts slow on the dummy.
syntaxExplanations.18: fast = dummy starts fast on the dummy.
syntaxExplanations.20: while moved < n: open an n-node gap.
syntaxExplanations.21: fast = nextp[fast] moves fast one node.
syntaxExplanations.23: while nextp[fast] != -1: slide until fast is the last node.
syntaxExplanations.24: slow = nextp[slow] moves slow one node.
syntaxExplanations.25: fast = nextp[fast] moves fast one node.
syntaxExplanations.26: removed = nextp[slow] is the node to delete.
syntaxExplanations.29: head = nextp[dummy] is the (possibly new) head.
syntaxExplanations.30: order = [] will collect the remaining values.
syntaxExplanations.32: while cur2 != -1: traverse to the end.
syntaxExplanations.34: cur2 = nextp[cur2] follows the next pointer.
syntaxExplanations.35: return order hands back the remaining values.
